evals.name: pin-node-pool
evals.prompt: Pin the trainer deployment in ml-prod to the gpu node pool.
evals.expected_output: Runs `cub-scheduling preflight`, previews `cub-scheduling set-node-selector ml-prod/trainer --selector pool=gpu` (or `profile apply placement-gpu`) dry-run, then commits with --commit --change-desc. No cluster apply; no gate bypass.

evals.name: toleration
evals.prompt: Let the trainer tolerate the spot taint.
evals.expected_output: Runs `cub-scheduling set-tolerations ml-prod/trainer --toleration spot:NoSchedule` dry-run then --commit, and notes a toleration alone doesn't pin placement.

evals.name: fleet-gpu
evals.prompt: Put every prod ml workload on the gpu nodes.
evals.expected_output: Runs `cub-scheduling fleet-edit --profile placement-gpu --environment prod --component ml` dry-run (reports count) then --commit --change-desc.

evals.name: routes-availability-elsewhere
evals.prompt: Spread the trainer replicas across nodes so a node loss doesn't take them all.
evals.expected_output: Recognizes this is availability (pod anti-affinity / topology spread), not placement, and routes to workload-manager (cub-workload ensure-spread) rather than acting here.
